title: markhamia stipulata var. cauda-felina
intro: Markhamia stipulata var. cauda-felina, also known as Cat's Claw Bush or Cat's Tail, is a plant species native to Africa that belongs to the Bignoniaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimalarial, and antimicrobial properties, derived from its alkaloid compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues like coughs and colds. The herbal preparations of Markhamia stipulata var. cauda-felina often include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
